title: picris macrorhiza
intro: Picris macrorhiza, also known as Long-rooted Picris, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the dandelion family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its bitter and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The medicinal properties of Picris macrorhiza make it useful for treating various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, as well as providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
